rationale_for_baseline_tier: Suspected acute aortic dissection with a hypertensive emergency in a patient with a known high-risk aortopathy (Turner syndrome, bicuspid aortic valve, coarctation of the aorta). Sudden tearing interscapular chest pain, pounding headache, faintness and severe breathlessness, with in-person findings of an arm-versus-leg blood-pressure gradient and radio-femoral delay, represent an immediate threat to life. T5 (call 000 for an emergency ambulance) is the only appropriate tier; the patient needs emergent CT aortography, IV blood-pressure control and urgent cardiothoracic surgery, and any exertion (including self-transport) can precipitate rupture.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening pain, increasing breathlessness, or feeling more faint before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Stay on the line with 000; keep completely still and upright
escalation_edges.rationale: Progression signals extension of the dissection or impending rupture; the only lever is to keep the patient still and maintain the emergency response.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain gets worse, your breathing gets harder, or you feel more faint, stay exactly where you are and keep the 000 operator on the line — help is on the way.

escalation_edges.rationale: Collapse indicates rupture, tamponade or arrest — the critical resource is paramedic resuscitation; lay her flat and be ready to start CPR under 000's guidance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she collapses or stops responding, tell 000 immediately, lay her flat, and begin CPR if she stops breathing and the operator guides you.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Turner syndrome with bicuspid aortic valve and coarctation of the aorta — a very high-risk aortopathy
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Chest pain in this patient must be treated as aortic dissection until proven otherwise; the threshold for 000 escalation is very low and does not require any remote 'confirmation'.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Young adult (26) — a demographic in which chest pain is often mislabelled as anxiety or musculoskeletal
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Do NOT let youth lower concern; age-based anchoring is the primary under-triage risk here. The known aortopathy overrides the low base rate of dissection in young adults.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 now and ask for an emergency (Priority 1) ambulance — tell them you have severe tearing chest pain and a known heart/aorta condition. Stay completely still and sitting upright; do not stand, walk, or lift anything. Take slow, shallow breaths. Unlock the front door for the paramedics and stay with your roommate. Tell the crew you have Turner syndrome, a bicuspid aortic valve and a coarctation of the aorta.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 immediately and request a Priority 1 ambulance
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary; ongoing cardiothoracic and cardiology follow-up and lifelong Turner-syndrome aortic surveillance should be arranged with the GP.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing tearing chest pain as anxiety, a panic attack, musculoskeletal strain, or 'part of Turner syndrome' and failing to call 000.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Undetected aortic dissection ruptures — the patient dies within minutes to hours.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising seriousness but advising self-transport to the emergency department rather than an ambulance — exertion of travelling increases aortic shear and can precipitate rupture en route without medical support or IV BP control.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot confirm dissection (no limb BP gradient, no auscultation, no imaging) or fully exclude an MI or PE.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate immediately on clinical suspicion — call 000; treat as an aortic catastrophe until hospital imaging proves otherwise, and avoid antiplatelet therapy.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Waiting for confirmation, seeking to 'verify' the diagnosis remotely, or empirically treating as an MI with aspirin.
